name: Metformin inhibited SASP through IKK/NF-kB-linked signaling in cell models
summary: This cell-model result explains why metformin belongs in senomorphics while keeping the direct evidence tier mechanistic.
statement: Metformin inhibited genes encoding multiple inflammatory cytokines in cellular senescence models, reduced senescent-cell conditioned-medium stimulation of prostate cancer cell growth, and interfered with NF-kB nuclear translocation and IKK/IkB phosphorylation.
population_or_model: Senescence-linked cell systems, AMPK-null fibroblasts, macrophages, and conditioned-media assays
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports AMPK-independent inhibition of NF-kB pathway activation in several cell contexts.
caveats: The source is mechanistic and cell-based.
Metformin effects on SASP cannot be cleanly separated from its broader nutrient-sensing, mitochondrial, and inflammatory actions in humans.
Cell-model SASP suppression does not establish systemic human functional benefit.
